items.q: What sport is best suited to type I (slow-twitch) fibres?
items.why: Students confuse which fibres suit which event. Type I = slow, fatigue-resistant, aerobic (endurance). Type II = fast, powerful, anaerobic (sprint/power). Distance runners are type-I-dominant.

items.q: What is the rough formula for maximum heart rate?
items.why: Students misremember the formula. 220 - age is the classic estimate (rough; varies individually). A 20-year-old's estimated max HR is around 200 bpm; an 80-year-old's is around 140.

items.q: When you flex your elbow, which muscle is the agonist?
items.why: Students confuse agonist (the prime mover doing the action) with antagonist (the opposing muscle that relaxes). In elbow flexion, biceps is the agonist; triceps is the antagonist (and vice versa for extension).

items.q: Unfair treatment based on race is?
items.why: It's Discrimination. “Etiquette” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.
Correct answer: Sportsmanship. “Hooliganism” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: How many 'protected characteristics' does the UK Equality Act 2010 list?
items.why: The real figure is 9 characteristics. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
